01Selected Projects
PROJECT 01
Solar Panel Deployment Mechanism for a 6U CubeSat

M.Sc. thesis at Aalto University for the Foresail-2 mission. Designed a cost-effective, passive mechanism to deploy two stacked solar panels in orbit for a 6U CubeSat bound for geostationary transfer orbit. The project involved a full product development cycle, from literature review and concept selection through detailed CAD to a working prototype. Only commercial off-the-shelf materials and university-accessible fabrication methods were used.

  • Defined functional, performance & interface requirements from mission parameters and CubeSat standards
  • Evaluated deployment concepts with a weighted decision matrix. Based on that selected a dual tape-spring hinge
  • Engineered the hinge: tape geometry, opposite-sense pairing for smooth torque, curvature-matched saddle blocks
  • Built the full parametric CAD model in Fusion 360 (hinge assembly, saddles, panel interfaces)
  • Fabricated & tested a full-scale prototype (laser-cut panels, 3D-printed saddles, steel tape-springs)
Fusion 360FDM 3D PrintingLaser CuttingMechanism DesignSpring Steel / Tape-Springs
180°
Reliable deployment

Cycles, no deformation

Stowed, full recovery
PROJECT 02
3D Scanning & Digital Metrology Feasibility Study — Vaisala R&D

Summer R&D project at Vaisala. The scope was to assess where 3D scanning, reverse engineering and digital inspection could create measurable value. Rather than scanning parts in isolation, the work mapped concrete engineering use cases. The case studies were legacy-part digitization, jig & fixture development, design verification and failure analysis. Also delivered a software recommendation plus a strategic adoption roadmap.

  • Reverse engineering: Complex production tooling and technologies were scanned. The scanned models were then used to reconstruct accurate parametric CAD models. The case study involved capturing critical dimensions for spare-part creation and supplier-independent redesign
  • Digital inspection: built scan-to-CAD alignment using digital inspection software that evaluate an entire part at once. It surfaced local problem areas instantly with quantifiable data
  • Failure analysis: developed a before/after drop-test method — scanning a component pre- and post-impact, aligning datasets and generating colour maps and cross-sections that quantified deformation against a ±1 mm tolerance where visual inspection alone was subjective
  • Fixture development: used scanned tool geometry to accelerate jig design and reduce manual measurement and iteration
  • Software benchmark: evaluated QuickSurface against Geomagic Design X across speed, stability, large-mesh handling and CAD integration. Recommended QuickSurface as the preferred reverse-engineering solution for its faster, more responsive SolidWorks-integrated workflow
Creaform 3D ScannerCreaform InspectionQuickSurfaceGeomagic Design XSolidWorksGD&T

PROJECT 03
CompositionUltra — Graphene-Membrane Gas Analyser

Aalto Design Factory product development project (MEC-E3001) for Megamorph, a graphene-display startup. As an 11-person team we were briefed to find a revolutionary new application for their GMOD graphene membrane. We settled on automated gas-composition measurement. The idea takes advantage of the fact that hydrogen and helium pass through porous graphene at different rates, while the membrane shows its state optically by changing the colour of reflected light. We built this into an inline device for industrial monitoring.

  • Helped develop the core technical concept, connecting gas permeation through porous graphene to a pressure-driven measurement that's read optically
  • Designed the aluminium device body and prepared CAD model. The product was a two-half machined enclosure with a heatsink exterior, a swappable membrane port and cavities for the internal components
  • Worked out the internal layout around the compression screw, solenoid valves, camera/LED unit and microcontroller, all within a 150 × 100 × 50 mm footprint
  • Led the prototyping and 3D printing, producing sectioned models and a finished demonstrator for the final exhibition
  • Helped shape the roadmap for further work: scaling up the porous graphene, optical characterisation, and a PLC/software interface for integrating the device into a plant
Solid EdgeCAD / Enclosure DesignSLA / FDM 3D PrintingRapid PrototypingCollaborative Product Development

PROJECT 04
Prosthetic Socket Fabrication — Scan to Print

Bachelor's thesis at UET Peshawar. Implemented a fully digital fabrication workflow for prosthetic sockets, replacing traditional manual mould-making. Patient limb geometry was 3D scanned, reconstructed into CAD, then 3D printed and tested directly on patients in a low-resource clinical setting — cutting production cost by 88% and production time by 90% while improving fit and comfort.

  • 3D scanned patient limb geometry as the basis for a custom socket
  • Reconstructed CAD models of the socket and components from scan data
  • Prepared and optimised models for FDM additive manufacturing
  • Fabricated & fitted printed sockets directly on patients, iterating on design and print parameters
SolidWorksFusion 3603D ScanningFDM 3D PrintingPrototyping

PROJECT 05
High-Resolution Additive Manufacturing Prototypes

Produced prototype components using high-resolution additive manufacturing. The work covered preparing CAD for print, fabricating fine-detail parts via MJP wax printing, and evaluating complex metal additive-manufactured components for design validation.

  • Prepared CAD models for additive manufacturing workflows
  • Fabricated high-resolution parts using MJP wax printing
  • Produced demonstration parts with fine mechanical detail
  • Evaluated complex metal additive-manufactured components
  • Supported rapid prototyping & design validation
SolidWorksFusion 3603D Systems MJP 300WProJet 1200Metal AM
PROJECT 06
Educational Cutaway Display Models — Atomic Energy Commission

The Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission handed our team a set of technical drawings and references and a deceptively simple brief: turn them into two life-sized cutaway models — a vertical multistage pump and a reactor-type pressure vessel — to stand behind glass in a public museum, teaching visitors, students and the commission's own engineers how the machines work inside. I helped take the project the whole way: first rebuilding the complete internal geometry of both machines in CAD from the supplied references, then planning and executing the fabrication strategy that turned those digital models into towering, colour-coded physical displays — the taller of the two standing well over head height.

  • Reconstructed the full internal architecture of both machines in CAD from client-supplied drawings and references
  • Engineered the build for fabrication: oversized bodies were split into printer-sized segments with integrated snap-clips and bonded joints, so dozens of printed sections reassembled into seamless full-scale structures
  • Produced the parts across mixed processes — FDM 3D printing for internals and shells, laser-cut plywood and acrylic for flat features, and CNC machining where precision mattered
  • Hand-finished and colour-coded every component to a defined scheme so each subsystem — shaft, impeller, bearings, core internals, tube sheet — reads clearly through the cutaway
  • Assembled and integrated both complete floor-standing models on custom welded stands, then crated them in foam-padded boxes for safe delivery
SolidWorksFDM 3D PrintingLaser CuttingCNC MachiningDesign for FabricationWorkshop Fabrication
